Evidence: index.html:7 <link rel=canonical href=/> — RELATIVE URL INVALID; seo.ts:189 runtime canonical is absolute via absUrl() ✓ (CSR only); Resources.tsx:109 blog index canonical always /resources regardless of language — no ES canonical

Evidence: seo.ts:192-201 hreflang cleared/re-set on each navigation ✓; LandingPageTemplate.tsx:27-33 bilingual alternates with x-default ✓; sitemap.xml correct xhtml:link entries ✓; Resources.tsx:223-244 no alternates key → default fallback sets all three hreflang to same canonical URL (FAIL); HowItWorks.tsx:10 same default fallback problem; seo.ts:193-198 default fallback incorrectly declares ES version at EN-only URLs
WhyItMatters: The default hreflang fallback actively misinforms Google about non-existent ES pages at EN-only URLs. Can cause Google to apply ES signals to EN-only pages and vice versa.
RecommendedFix: (1) Remove 3-hreflang default fallback; replace with en+x-default for EN-only pages. (2) Add /es/recursos route. (3) Consider /es/recursos/:slug for bilingual blog posts.

Evidence: Build: hero-portrait.jpg 1546.70 kB; logo.png 1482.35 kB; index.js 1404.31 kB (gzip 404 kB); Home.tsx:8 TODO comment confirms known unfixed hero size; vite.config.ts no rollupOptions.output.manualChunks; Home.tsx:73 hero img no fetchPriority=high; LandingPageTemplate.tsx:69 has fetchPriority=high ✓; Estimated LCP on homepage mobile: 6-10s
WhyItMatters: Core Web Vitals are a direct Google ranking signal since 2021. 1.5 MB hero causes critical LCP failure. 1.4 MB JS bundle delays TTI and INP. Google suppresses pages with poor CWV scores.
RecommendedFix: (1) Convert hero-portrait.jpg to WebP <100KB. (2) Compress logo to WebP/SVG <20KB. (3) Add fetchPriority=high + <link rel=preload as=image> for homepage LCP. (4) Add Vite manualChunks splitting vendor; router; supabase packages. (5) Use dynamic import() for admin/app routes.

Evidence:
CSR-only SPA: vite.config.ts — no SSG/SSR config; index.html:9
— blank content pre-JS; public/_redirects — no /* /index.html 200 catch-all → direct URL access to /meet-venezuelan-women may return 404 before React Router intercepts; Googlebot executes JS but with latency; Bing/DDG/social crawlers have partial JS execution; _redirects:1 sitemap shadow issue (see C06)
WhyItMatters: Without SPA catch-all redirect; every non-root URL may return 404 for non-JS crawlers. Without SSR/prerendering; indexation depends entirely on Googlebot JS rendering pipeline (5-10 day delay per page).
RecommendedFix: (1) Add /* /index.html 200 to _redirects immediately. (2) Add prerendering for top 10 landing pages via vite-plugin-prerender or Netlify prerendering. (3) Long-term: evaluate SSG with Astro or Vite SSR.

Evidence: No /about route in App.tsx; No team page; ResourceDetail.tsx:166 all blog authors: Organization — no named expert; StructuredData.tsx:12 sameAs: []; No press coverage links; No third-party trust certifications; No visible datePublished on landing pages; Legal.tsx:74 effective date 2026-01-01 shown ✓; Anti-solicitation policy exists ✓ (rare; positive); Safety page exists ✓; FAQ with 20 categorized Q&As ✓; YMYL classification — Google applies stricter E-E-A-T to dating sites
WhyItMatters: Google Quality Rater Guidelines require YMYL pages to demonstrate real expertise and trust. An international dating site with no About page; no named staff; and anonymous authorship will face skepticism in manual reviews.
RecommendedFix: (1) Create /about page with company story; mission; moderation team bio. (2) Add expert author persona to blog posts. (3) Add sameAs social links. (4) Add trust badge section. (5) Add visible datePublished and dateModified to blog posts.

RecommendedFix: URL-based pagination (?page=2) or ensure all post slugs stay in the sitemap as it grows.
